content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: Opinion Request Meeting Participants

Date: 7-1-77

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Ted Prim

Subject: Pension funds and income reporting.

At the opinion request meeting of June 13, 1977, the staff concluded that the interim advice contained in Ken Goshorn's memo of December 21, 1976, with respect to Federal pensions is no longer correct in light of the Moore opinion and the defeat of certain legislative amendments to the Act. Accordingly, payments from Federal pensions are reportable income. Payments of State and local pensions, on the other hand, are excluded from income by 82030(b)(2) and the Moore opinion. Payments from private pensions continue to be reportable income.

TP:jo
